NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David the servant of the LORD, who spoke to the LORD the words of this song on the day that the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. And he said: I will love You, O LORD, my strength.
NKJV.2: The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer; My God, my strength, in whom I will trust; My shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
NKJV.3: I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised; So shall I be saved from my enemies.
NKJV.4: The pangs of death surrounded me, And the floods of ungodliness made me afraid.
NKJV.5: The sorrows of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
NKJV.6: In my distress I called upon the LORD, And cried out to my God; He heard my voice from His temple, And my cry came before Him, even to His ears.
NKJV.7: Then the earth shook and trembled; The foundations of the hills also quaked and were shaken, Because He was angry.
NKJV.8: Smoke went up from His nostrils, And devouring fire from His mouth; Coals were kindled by it.
NKJV.9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down With darkness under His feet.
NKJV.10: And He rode upon a cherub, and flew;  He flew upon the wings of the wind.
NKJV.11: He made darkness His secret place; His canopy around Him was dark waters And thick clouds of the skies.
NKJV.12: From the brightness before Him, His thick clouds passed with hailstones and coals of fire.
NKJV.13: The LORD thundered from heaven, And the Most High uttered His voice, Hailstones and coals of fire.
NKJV.14: He sent out His arrows and scattered the foe, Lightnings in abundance, and He vanquished them.
NKJV.15: Then the channels of the sea were seen, The foundations of the world were uncovered At Your rebuke, O LORD, At the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
NKJV.16: He sent from above, He took me; He drew me out of many waters.
NKJV.17: He delivered me from my strong enemy, From those who hated me, For they were too strong for me.
NKJV.18: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, But the LORD was my support.
NKJV.19: He also brought me out into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
NKJV.20: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
NKJV.21: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, And have not wickedly departed from my God.
NKJV.22: For all His judgments were before me, And I did not put away His statutes from me.
NKJV.23: I was also blameless before Him, And I kept myself from my iniquity.
NKJV.24: Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, According to the cleanness of my hands in His sight.
NKJV.25: With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; With a blameless man You will show Yourself blameless;
NKJV.26: With the pure You will show Yourself pure; And with the devious You will show Yourself shrewd.
NKJV.27: For You will save the humble people, But will bring down haughty looks.
NKJV.28: For You will light my lamp; The LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.
NKJV.29: For by You I can run against a troop, By my God I can leap over a wall.
NKJV.30: As for God, His way is perfect; The word of the LORD is proven; He is a shield to all who trust in Him.
NKJV.31: For who is God, except the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God?
NKJV.32: It is God who arms me with strength, And makes my way perfect.
NKJV.33: He makes my feet like the feet of deer, And sets me on my high places.
NKJV.34: He teaches my hands to make war, So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
NKJV.35: You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; Your right hand has held me up, Your gentleness has made me great.
NKJV.36: You enlarged my path under me,  So my feet did not slip.
NKJV.37: I have pursued my enemies and overtaken them; Neither did I turn back again till they were destroyed.
NKJV.38: I have wounded them, So that they could not rise; They have fallen under my feet.
NKJV.39: For You have armed me with strength for the battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
NKJV.40: You have also given me the necks of my enemies, So that I destroyed those who hated me.
NKJV.41: They cried out, but there was none to save; Even to the LORD, but He did not answer them.
NKJV.42: Then I beat them as fine as the dust before the wind; I cast them out like dirt in the streets.
NKJV.43: You have delivered me from the strivings of the people;  You have made me the head of the nations;  A people I have not known shall serve me.
NKJV.44: As soon as they hear of me they obey me; The foreigners submit to me.
NKJV.45: The foreigners fade away, And come frightened from their hideouts.
NKJV.46: The LORD lives! Blessed be my Rock! Let the God of my salvation be exalted.
NKJV.47: It is God who avenges me, And subdues the peoples under me;
NKJV.48: He delivers me from my enemies.  You also lift me up above those who rise against me; You have delivered me from the violent man.
NKJV.49: Therefore I will give thanks to You, O LORD, among the Gentiles, And sing praises to Your name.
NKJV.50: Great deliverance He gives to His king, And shows mercy to His anointed, To David and his descendants forevermore.

ESV.1: I love you, O LORD, my strength.
ESV.2: The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer, my God, my rock, in whom I take refuge, my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
ESV.3: I call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised, and I am saved from my enemies.
ESV.4: The cords of death encompassed me; the torrents of destruction assailed me;
ESV.5: the cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
ESV.6: In my distress I called upon the LORD; to my God I cried for help. From his temple he heard my voice, and my cry to him reached his ears.
ESV.7: Then the earth reeled and rocked; the foundations also of the mountains trembled and quaked, because he was angry.
ESV.8: Smoke went up from his nostrils,  and devouring fire from his mouth; glowing coals flamed forth from him.
ESV.9: He bowed the heavens and came down; thick darkness was under his feet.
ESV.10: He rode on a cherub and flew; he came swiftly on the wings of the wind.
ESV.11: He made darkness his covering, his canopy around him, thick clouds dark with water.
ESV.12: Out of the brightness before him hailstones and coals of fire broke through his clouds.
ESV.13: The LORD also thundered in the heavens, and the Most High uttered his voice, hailstones and coals of fire.
ESV.14: And he sent out his arrows and scattered them; he flashed forth lightnings and routed them.
ESV.15: Then the channels of the sea were seen, and the foundations of the world were laid bare at your rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
ESV.16: He sent from on high, he took me; he drew me out of many waters.
ESV.17: He rescued me from my strong enemy and from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
ESV.18: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my support.
ESV.19: He brought me out into a broad place; he rescued me, because he delighted in me.
ESV.20: The LORD dealt with me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands he rewarded me.
ESV.21: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
ESV.22: For all his rules  were before me, and his statutes I did not put away from me.
ESV.23: I was blameless before him, and I kept myself from my guilt.
ESV.24: So the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
ESV.25: With the merciful you show yourself merciful; with the blameless man you show yourself blameless;
ESV.26: with the purified you show yourself pure; and with the crooked you make yourself seem tortuous.
ESV.27: For you save a humble people, but the haughty eyes you bring down.
ESV.28: For it is you who light my lamp; the LORD my God lightens my darkness.
ESV.29: For by you I can run against a troop, and by my God I can leap over a wall.
ESV.30: This God — his way is perfect;  the word of the LORD proves true; he is a shield for all those who take refuge in him.
ESV.31: For who is God, but the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God? —
ESV.32: the God who equipped me with strength and made my way blameless.
ESV.33: He made my feet like the feet of a deer and set me secure on the heights.
ESV.34: He trains my hands for war, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
ESV.35: You have given me the shield of your salvation, and your right hand supported me, and your gentleness made me great.
ESV.36: You gave a wide place for my steps under me, and my feet did not slip.
ESV.37: I pursued my enemies and overtook them, and did not turn back till they were consumed.
ESV.38: I thrust them through, so that they were not able to rise; they fell under my feet.
ESV.39: For you equipped me with strength for the battle; you made those who rise against me sink under me.
ESV.40: You made my enemies turn their backs to me,  and those who hated me I destroyed.
ESV.41: They cried for help, but there was none to save; they cried to the LORD, but he did not answer them.
ESV.42: I beat them fine as dust before the wind; I cast them out like the mire of the streets.
ESV.43: You delivered me from strife with the people; you made me the head of the nations; people whom I had not known served me.
ESV.44: As soon as they heard of me they obeyed me; foreigners came cringing to me.
ESV.45: Foreigners lost heart and came trembling out of their fortresses.
ESV.46: The LORD lives, and blessed be my rock, and exalted be the God of my salvation —
ESV.47: the God who gave me vengeance and subdued peoples under me,
ESV.48: who rescued me from my enemies; yes, you exalted me above those who rose against me; you delivered me from the man of violence.
ESV.49: For this I will praise you, O LORD, among the nations, and sing to your name.
ESV.50: Great salvation he brings to his king, and shows steadfast love to his anointed, to David and his offspring forever.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A [Psalm] of David the servant of the LORD, who spoke to the LORD the words of this song in the day that the LORD delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. And he said, 'I love You, O LORD, my strength.'
NASB.2: The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer, My God, my rock, in whom I take refuge; My shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
NASB.3: I call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised, And I am saved from my enemies.
NASB.4: The cords of death encompassed me, And the torrents of ungodliness terrified me.
NASB.5: The cords of Sheol surrounded me; The snares of death confronted me.
NASB.6: In my distress I called upon the LORD, And cried to my God for help; He heard my voice out of His temple, And my cry for help before Him came into His ears.
NASB.7: Then the earth shook and quaked; And the foundations of the mountains were trembling And were shaken, because He was angry.
NASB.8: Smoke went up out of His nostrils, And fire from His mouth devoured; Coals were kindled by it.
NASB.9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down With thick darkness under His feet.
NASB.10: He rode upon a cherub and flew; And He sped upon the wings of the wind.
NASB.11: He made darkness His hiding place, His canopy around Him, Darkness of waters, thick clouds of the skies.
NASB.12: From the brightness before Him passed His thick clouds, Hailstones and coals of fire.
NASB.13: The LORD also thundered in the heavens, And the Most High uttered His voice, Hailstones and coals of fire.
NASB.14: He sent out His arrows, and scattered them, And lightning flashes in abundance, and routed them.
NASB.15: Then the channels of water appeared, And the foundations of the world were laid bare At Your rebuke, O LORD, At the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
NASB.16: He sent from on high, He took me; He drew me out of many waters.
NASB.17: He delivered me from my strong enemy, And from those who hated me, for they were too mighty for me.
NASB.18: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, But the LORD was my stay.
NASB.19: He brought me forth also into a broad place; He rescued me, because He delighted in me.
NASB.20: The LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
NASB.21: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, And have not wickedly departed from my God.
NASB.22: For all His ordinances were before me, And I did not put away His statutes from me.
NASB.23: I was also blameless with Him, And I kept myself from my iniquity.
NASB.24: Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, According to the cleanness of my hands in His eyes.
NASB.25: With the kind You show Yourself kind; With the blameless You show Yourself blameless;
NASB.26: With the pure You show Yourself pure, And with the crooked You show Yourself astute.
NASB.27: For You save an afflicted people, But haughty eyes You abase.
NASB.28: For You light my lamp; The LORD my God illumines my darkness.
NASB.29: For by You I can run upon a troop; And by my God I can leap over a wall.
NASB.30: As for God, His way is blameless; The word of the LORD is tried; He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.
NASB.31: For who is God, but the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God,
NASB.32: The God who girds me with strength And makes my way blameless?
NASB.33: He makes my feet like hinds' [feet], And sets me upon my high places.
NASB.34: He trains my hands for battle, So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
NASB.35: You have also given me the shield of Your salvation, And Your right hand upholds me; And Your gentleness makes me great.
NASB.36: You enlarge my steps under me, And my feet have not slipped.
NASB.37: I pursued my enemies and overtook them, And I did not turn back until they were consumed.
NASB.38: I shattered them, so that they were not able to rise; They fell under my feet.
NASB.39: For You have girded me with strength for battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
NASB.40: You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me, And I destroyed those who hated me.
NASB.41: They cried for help, but there was none to save, [Even] to the LORD, but He did not answer them.
NASB.42: Then I beat them fine as the dust before the wind; I emptied them out as the mire of the streets.
NASB.43: You have delivered me from the contentions of the people; You have placed me as head of the nations; A people whom I have not known serve me.
NASB.44: As soon as they hear, they obey me; Foreigners submit to me.
NASB.45: Foreigners fade away, And come trembling out of their fortresses.
NASB.46: The LORD lives, and blessed be my rock; And exalted be the God of my salvation,
NASB.47: The God who executes vengeance for me, And subdues peoples under me.
NASB.48: He delivers me from my enemies; Surely You lift me above those who rise up against me; You rescue me from the violent man.
NASB.49: Therefore I will give thanks to You among the nations, O LORD, And I will sing praises to Your name.
NASB.50: He gives great deliverance to His king, And shows lovingkindness to His anointed, To David and his descendants forever.

NLT.1:
I love you, LORD;
you are my strength.
NLT.2:
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my savior;
my God is my rock, in whom I find protection.
He is my shield, the power that saves me,
and my place of safety.
NLT.3:
I called on the LORD, who is worthy of praise,
and he saved me from my enemies.
NLT.4:
The ropes of death entangled me;
floods of destruction swept over me.
NLT.5:
The grave wrapped its ropes around me;
death laid a trap in my path.
NLT.6:
But in my distress I cried out to the LORD;
yes, I prayed to my God for help.
He heard me from his sanctuary;
my cry to him reached his ears.
NLT.7:
Then the earth quaked and trembled.
The foundations of the mountains shook;
they quaked because of his anger.
NLT.8:
Smoke poured from his nostrils;
fierce flames leaped from his mouth.
Glowing coals blazed forth from him.
NLT.9:
He opened the heavens and came down;
dark storm clouds were beneath his feet.
NLT.10:
Mounted on a mighty angelic being, he flew,
soaring on the wings of the wind.
NLT.11:
He shrouded himself in darkness,
veiling his approach with dark rain clouds.
NLT.12:
Thick clouds shielded the brightness around him
and rained down hail and burning coals.
NLT.13:
The LORD thundered from heaven;
the voice of the Most High resounded
amid the hail and burning coals.
NLT.14:
He shot his arrows and scattered his enemies;
great bolts of lightning flashed, and they were confused.
NLT.15:
Then at your command, O LORD,
at the blast of your breath,
the bottom of the sea could be seen,
and the foundations of the earth were laid bare.
NLT.16:
He reached down from heaven and rescued me;
he drew me out of deep waters.
NLT.17:
He rescued me from my powerful enemies,
from those who hated me and were too strong for me.
NLT.18:
They attacked me at a moment when I was in distress,
but the LORD supported me.
NLT.19:
He led me to a place of safety;
he rescued me because he delights in me.
NLT.20:
The LORD rewarded me for doing right;
he restored me because of my innocence.
NLT.21:
For I have kept the ways of the LORD;
I have not turned from my God to follow evil.
NLT.22:
I have followed all his regulations;
I have never abandoned his decrees.
NLT.23:
I am blameless before God;
I have kept myself from sin.
NLT.24:
The LORD rewarded me for doing right.
He has seen my innocence.
NLT.25:
To the faithful you show yourself faithful;
to those with integrity you show integrity.
NLT.26:
To the pure you show yourself pure,
but to the crooked you show yourself shrewd.
NLT.27:
You rescue the humble,
but you humiliate the proud.
NLT.28:
You light a lamp for me.
The LORD, my God, lights up my darkness.
NLT.29:
In your strength I can crush an army;
with my God I can scale any wall.
NLT.30:
God’s way is perfect.
All the LORD’s promises prove true.
He is a shield for all who look to him for protection.
NLT.31:
For who is God except the LORD?
Who but our God is a solid rock?
NLT.32:
God arms me with strength,
and he makes my way perfect.
NLT.33:
He makes me as surefooted as a deer,
enabling me to stand on mountain heights.
NLT.34:
He trains my hands for battle;
he strengthens my arm to draw a bronze bow.
NLT.35:
You have given me your shield of victory.
Your right hand supports me;
your help has made me great.
NLT.36:
You have made a wide path for my feet
to keep them from slipping.
NLT.37:
I chased my enemies and caught them;
I did not stop until they were conquered.
NLT.38:
I struck them down so they could not get up;
they fell beneath my feet.
NLT.39:
You have armed me with strength for the battle;
you have subdued my enemies under my feet.
NLT.40:
You placed my foot on their necks.
I have destroyed all who hated me.
NLT.41:
They called for help, but no one came to their rescue.
They even cried to the LORD, but he refused to answer.
NLT.42:
I ground them as fine as dust in the wind.
I swept them into the gutter like dirt.
NLT.43:
You gave me victory over my accusers.
You appointed me ruler over nations;
people I don’t even know now serve me.
NLT.44:
As soon as they hear of me, they submit;
foreign nations cringe before me.
NLT.45:
They all lose their courage
and come trembling from their strongholds.
NLT.46:
The LORD lives! Praise to my Rock!
May the God of my salvation be exalted!
NLT.47:
He is the God who pays back those who harm me;
he subdues the nations under me
NLT.48:
and rescues me from my enemies.
You hold me safe beyond the reach of my enemies;
you save me from violent opponents.
NLT.49:
For this, O LORD, I will praise you among the nations;
I will sing praises to your name.
NLT.50:
You give great victories to your king;
you show unfailing love to your anointed,
to David and all his descendants forever.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By David the servant of Yahweh, who spoke to Yahweh the words of this song in the day that Yahweh delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul. He said, I love you, Yahweh, my strength.
WEB.2: Yahweh is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my rock, in whom I take refuge; my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower.
WEB.3: I call on Yahweh, who is worthy to be praised; and I am saved from my enemies.
WEB.4: The cords of death surrounded me. The floods of ungodliness made me afraid.
WEB.5: The cords of Sheol  were around me. The snares of death came on me.
WEB.6: In my distress I called on Yahweh, and cried to my God. He heard my voice out of his temple. My cry before him came into his ears.
WEB.7: Then the earth shook and trembled. The foundations also of the mountains quaked and were shaken, because he was angry.
WEB.8: Smoke went out of his nostrils. Consuming fire came out of his mouth. Coals were kindled by it.
WEB.9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down. Thick darkness was under his feet.
WEB.10: He rode on a cherub, and flew. Yes, he soared on the wings of the wind.
WEB.11: He made darkness his hiding place, his pavilion around him, darkness of waters, thick clouds of the skies.
WEB.12: At the brightness before him his thick clouds passed, hailstones and coals of fire.
WEB.13: Yahweh also thundered in the sky. The Most High uttered his voice: hailstones and coals of fire.
WEB.14: He sent out his arrows, and scattered them; Yes, great lightning bolts, and routed them.
WEB.15: Then the channels of waters appeared. The foundations of the world were laid bare at your rebuke, Yahweh, at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
WEB.16: He sent from on high. He took me. He drew me out of many waters.
WEB.17: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me; for they were too mighty for me.
WEB.18: They came on me in the day of my calamity, but Yahweh was my support.
WEB.19: He brought me out also into a large place. He delivered me, because he delighted in me.
WEB.20: Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness. According to the cleanness of my hands has he recompensed me.
WEB.21: For I have kept the ways of Yahweh, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
WEB.22: For all his ordinances were before me. I didn’t put away his statutes from me.
WEB.23: I was also blameless with him. I kept myself from my iniquity.
WEB.24: Therefore Yahweh has rewarded me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in his eyesight.
WEB.25: With the merciful you will show yourself merciful. With the perfect man, you will show yourself perfect.
WEB.26: With the pure, you will show yourself pure. With the crooked you will show yourself shrewd.
WEB.27: For you will save the afflicted people, but the haughty eyes you will bring down.
WEB.28: For you will light my lamp, Yahweh. My God will light up my darkness.
WEB.29: For by you, I advance through a troop. By my God, I leap over a wall.
WEB.30: As for God, his way is perfect. Yahweh’s word is tried. He is a shield to all those who take refuge in him.
WEB.31: For who is God, except Yahweh? Who is a rock, besides our God,
WEB.32: the God who arms me with strength, and makes my way perfect?
WEB.33: He makes my feet like deer’s feet, and sets me on my high places.
WEB.34: He teaches my hands to war, so that my arms bend a bow of bronze.
WEB.35: You have also given me the shield of your salvation. Your right hand sustains me. Your gentleness has made me great.
WEB.36: You have enlarged my steps under me, My feet have not slipped.
WEB.37: I will pursue my enemies, and overtake them. Neither will I turn again until they are consumed.
WEB.38: I will strike them through, so that they will not be able to rise. They shall fall under my feet.
WEB.39: For you have armed me with strength to the battle. You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
WEB.40: You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me, that I might cut off those who hate me.
WEB.41: They cried, but there was no one to save; even to Yahweh, but he didn’t answer them.
WEB.42: Then I beat them small as the dust before the wind. I cast them out as the mire of the streets.
WEB.43: You have delivered me from the strivings of the people. You have made me the head of the nations. A people whom I have not known shall serve me.
WEB.44: As soon as they hear of me they shall obey me. The foreigners shall submit themselves to me.
WEB.45: The foreigners shall fade away, and shall come trembling out of their close places.
WEB.46: Yahweh lives; and blessed be my rock. Exalted be the God of my salvation,
WEB.47: even the God who executes vengeance for me, and subdues peoples under me.
WEB.48: He rescues me from my enemies. Yes, you lift me up above those who rise up against me. You deliver me from the violent man.
WEB.49: Therefore I will give thanks to you, Yahweh, among the nations, and will sing praises to your name.
WEB.50: He gives great deliverance to his king, and shows loving kindness to his anointed, to David and to his offspring,  forever more.

AMP.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David, the servant of the LORD, who spoke the words of this song to the LORD on the day when the LORD rescued him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. And he said: "I love You [fervently and devotedly], O LORD, my strength."
AMP.2:
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and the One who rescues me;
My God, my rock and strength in whom I trust and take refuge;
My shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower—my stronghold.
AMP.3:
I call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised;
And I am saved from my enemies.
AMP.4:
The cords of death surrounded me,
And the streams of ungodliness and torrents of destruction terrified me.
AMP.5:
The cords of Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) surrounded me;
The snares of death confronted me.
AMP.6:
In my distress [when I seemed surrounded] I called upon the LORD
And cried to my God for help;
He heard my voice from His temple,
And my cry for help came before Him, into His very ears.
AMP.7:
Then the earth shook and quaked,
The foundations of the mountains trembled;
They were shaken because He was indignant and angry.
AMP.8:
Smoke went up from His nostrils,
And fire from His mouth devoured;
Coals were kindled by it.
AMP.9:
He bowed the heavens also and came down;
And thick darkness was under His feet.
AMP.10:
And He rode upon a cherub (storm) and flew;
And He sped on the wings of the wind.
AMP.11:
He made darkness His hiding place (covering); His pavilion (canopy) around Him,
The darkness of the waters, the thick clouds of the skies.
AMP.12:
Out of the brightness before Him passed His thick clouds,
Hailstones and coals of fire.
AMP.13:
The LORD also thundered in the heavens,
And the Most High uttered His voice,
Hailstones and coals of fire.
AMP.14:
He sent out His arrows and scattered them;
And He sent an abundance of lightning flashes and confused and routed them [in defeat].
AMP.15:
Then the stream beds of the waters appeared,
And the foundations of the world were laid bare
At Your rebuke, O LORD,
At the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
AMP.16:
He reached from on high, He took me;
He drew me out of many waters.
AMP.17:
He rescued me from my strong enemy,
And from those who hated me, for they were too strong for me.
AMP.18:
They confronted me in the day of my disaster,
But the LORD was my support.
AMP.19:
He brought me out into a broad place;
He rescued me because He was pleased with me and delighted in me.
AMP.20:
The LORD dealt with me according to my righteousness (moral character, spiritual integrity);
According to the cleanness of my hands He has rewarded me.
AMP.21:
For I have kept the ways of the LORD,
And have not wickedly departed from my God.
AMP.22:
For all His ordinances were before me,
And I did not put away His statutes from me.
AMP.23:
I was blameless before Him,
And I kept myself free from my sin.
AMP.24:
Therefore the LORD has rewarded me according to my righteousness (moral character, spiritual integrity),
According to the cleanness of my hands in His sight.
AMP.25:
With the kind (merciful, faithful, loyal) You show Yourself kind,
With the blameless You show Yourself blameless,
AMP.26:
With the pure You show Yourself pure,
And with the crooked You show Yourself astute.
AMP.27:
For You save an afflicted and humble people,
But bring down those [arrogant fools] with haughty eyes.
AMP.28:
For You cause my lamp to be lighted and to shine;
The LORD my God illumines my darkness.
AMP.29:
For by You I can crush a troop,
And by my God I can leap over a wall.
AMP.30:
As for God, His way is blameless.
The word of the LORD is tested [it is perfect, it is faultless];
He is a shield to all who take refuge in Him.
AMP.31:
For who is God, but the LORD?
Or who is a rock, except our God,
AMP.32:
The God who encircles me with strength
And makes my way blameless?
AMP.33:
He makes my feet like hinds' feet [able to stand firmly and tread safely on paths of testing and trouble];
He sets me [securely] upon my high places.
AMP.34:
He trains my hands for war,
So that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
AMP.35:
You have also given me the shield of Your salvation,
And Your right hand upholds and sustains me;
Your gentleness [Your gracious response when I pray] makes me great.
AMP.36:
You enlarge the path beneath me and make my steps secure,
So that my feet will not slip.
AMP.37:
I pursued my enemies and overtook them;
And I did not turn back until they were consumed.
AMP.38:
I shattered them so that they were not able to rise;
They fell [wounded] under my feet.
AMP.39:
For You have encircled me with strength for the battle;
You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
AMP.40:
You have also made my enemies turn their backs to me [in defeat],
And I silenced and destroyed those who hated me.
AMP.41:
They cried for help, but there was no one to save them—
Even to the LORD [they cried], but He did not answer them.
AMP.42:
Then I beat them fine as the dust before the wind;
I emptied them out as the dirt of the streets.
AMP.43:
You have rescued me from the contentions of the people;
You have placed me as the head of the nations;
A people whom I have not known serve me.
AMP.44:
As soon as they hear me, they respond and obey me;
Foreigners feign obedience to me.
AMP.45:
Foreigners lose heart,
And come trembling out of their strongholds.
AMP.46:
The LORD lives, blessed be my rock;
And may the God of my salvation be exalted,
AMP.47:
The God who avenges me,
And subdues peoples (nations) under me.
AMP.48:
He rescues me from my enemies;
Yes, You lift me up above those who rise up against me;
You deliver me from the man of violence.
AMP.49:
Therefore will I give thanks and praise You, O LORD, among the nations,
And sing praises to Your name.
AMP.50:
He gives great triumphs to His king,
And shows steadfast love and mercy to His anointed,
To David and his descendants forever.

MSG.1:
A David song, which he sang to God after being saved from all his enemies and from Saul.
I love you, God—
you make me strong.
MSG.2:
God is bedrock under my feet,
the castle in which I live,
my rescuing knight.
My God—the high crag
where I run for dear life,
hiding behind the boulders,
safe in the granite hideout.
MSG.3:
I sing to God, the Praise-Lofty,
and find myself safe and saved.
MSG.4:
The hangman's noose was tight at my throat;
devil waters rushed over me.
MSG.5:
Hell's ropes cinched me tight;
death traps barred every exit.
MSG.6:
A hostile world! I call to God,
I cry to God to help me.
From his palace he hears my call;
my cry brings me right into his presence—
a private audience!
MSG.7:
Earth wobbles and lurches;
huge mountains shake like leaves,
Quake like aspen leaves
because of his rage.
MSG.8:
His nostrils flare, bellowing smoke;
his mouth spits fire.
Tongues of fire dart in and out;
MSG.9:
he lowers the sky.
He steps down;
under his feet an abyss opens up.
MSG.10:
He's riding a winged creature,
swift on wind-wings.
MSG.11:
Now he's wrapped himself
in a trenchcoat of black-cloud darkness.
MSG.12:
But his cloud-brightness bursts through,
spraying hailstones and fireballs.
MSG.13:
Then God thundered out of heaven;
the High God gave a great shout,
spraying hailstones and fireballs.
MSG.14:
God shoots his arrows—pandemonium!
He hurls his lightnings—a rout!
MSG.15:
The secret sources of ocean are exposed,
the hidden depths of earth lie uncovered
The moment you roar in protest,
let loose your hurricane anger.
MSG.16:
But me he caught—reached all the way
from sky to sea; he pulled me out
Of that ocean of hate,
MSG.17:
that enemy chaos,
the void in which I was drowning.
MSG.18:
They hit me when I was down,
but God stuck by me.
MSG.19:
He stood me up on a wide-open field;
I stood there saved—surprised to be loved!
MSG.20:
God made my life complete
when I placed all the pieces before him.
When I got my act together,
he gave me a fresh start.
MSG.21:
Now I'm alert to God's ways;
I don't take God for granted.
MSG.22:
Every day I review the ways he works;
I try not to miss a trick.
MSG.23:
I feel put back together,
and I'm watching my step.
MSG.24:
God rewrote the text of my life
when I opened the book of my heart to his eyes.
MSG.25:
The good people taste your goodness,
The whole people taste your health,
MSG.26:
The true people taste your truth,
The bad ones can't figure you out.
MSG.27:
You take the side of the down-and-out,
But the stuck-up you take down a peg.
MSG.28:
Suddenly, God, you floodlight my life;
I'm blazing with glory, God's glory!
MSG.29:
I smash the bands of marauders,
I vault the highest fences.
MSG.30:
What a God! His road
stretches straight and smooth.
Every God-direction is road-tested.
Everyone who runs toward him
Makes it.
MSG.31:
Is there any god like God?
Are we not at bedrock?
MSG.32:
Is not this the God who armed me,
then aimed me in the right direction?
MSG.33:
Now I run like a deer;
I'm king of the mountain.
MSG.34:
He shows me how to fight;
I can bend a bronze bow!
MSG.35:
You protect me with salvation-armor;
you hold me up with a firm hand,
caress me with your gentle ways.
MSG.36:
You cleared the ground under me
so my footing was firm.
MSG.37:
When I chased my enemies I caught them;
I didn't let go till they were dead men.
MSG.38:
I nailed them; they were down for good;
then I walked all over them.
MSG.39:
You armed me well for this fight,
you smashed the upstarts.
MSG.40:
You made my enemies turn tail,
and I wiped out the haters.
MSG.41:
They cried "uncle"
but Uncle didn't come;
They yelled for God
and got no for an answer.
MSG.42:
I ground them to dust; they gusted in the wind.
I threw them out, like garbage in the gutter.
MSG.43:
You rescued me from a squabbling people;
you made me a leader of nations.
People I'd never heard of served me;
MSG.44:
the moment they got wind of me they listened.
The foreign devils gave up;
MSG.45:
they came
on their bellies, crawling from their hideouts.
MSG.46:
Live, God! Blessings from my Rock,
my free and freeing God, towering!
MSG.47:
This God set things right for me
and shut up the people who talked back.
MSG.48:
He rescued me from enemy anger,
he pulled me from the grip of upstarts,
He saved me from the bullies.
MSG.49:
That's why I'm thanking you, God,
all over the world.
That's why I'm singing songs
that rhyme your name.
MSG.50:
God's king takes the trophy;
God's chosen is beloved.
I mean David and all his children—
always.

NIV.1:
Psalm 18
For the director of music. Of David the servant of the Lord. He sang to the Lord the words of this song when the Lord delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul. He said:
I love you, O Lord, my strength.
NIV.2:
The Lord is my rock, my fortress and my deliverer;
my God is my rock, in whom I take refuge.
He is my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
NIV.3:
I call to the Lord, who is worthy of praise,
and I am saved from my enemies.
NIV.4:
The cords of death entangled me;
the torrents of destruction overwhelmed me.
NIV.5:
The cords of the grave coiled around me;
the snares of death confronted me.
NIV.6:
In my distress I called to the Lord;
I cried to my God for help.
From his temple he heard my voice;
my cry came before him, into his ears.
NIV.7:
The earth trembled and quaked,
and the foundations of the mountains shook;
they trembled because he was angry.
NIV.8:
Smoke rose from his nostrils;
consuming fire came from his mouth,
burning coals blazed out of it.
NIV.9:
He parted the heavens and came down;
dark clouds were under his feet.
NIV.10:
He mounted the cherubim and flew;
he soared on the wings of the wind.
NIV.11:
He made darkness his covering, his canopy around him—
the dark rain clouds of the sky.
NIV.12:
Out of the brightness of his presence clouds advanced,
with hailstones and bolts of lightning.
NIV.13:
The Lord thundered from heaven;
the voice of the Most High resounded.
NIV.14:
He shot his arrows and scattered the enemies,
great bolts of lightning and routed them.
NIV.15:
The valleys of the sea were exposed
and the foundations of the earth laid bare
at your rebuke, O Lord,
at the blast of breath from your nostrils.
NIV.16:
He reached down from on high and took hold of me;
he drew me out of deep waters.
NIV.17:
He rescued me from my powerful enemy,
from my foes, who were too strong for me.
NIV.18:
They confronted me in the day of my disaster,
but the Lord was my support.
NIV.19:
He brought me out into a spacious place;
he rescued me because he delighted in me.
NIV.20:
The Lord has dealt with me according to my righteousness;
according to the cleanness of my hands he has rewarded me.
NIV.21:
For I have kept the ways of the Lord;
I have not done evil by turning from my God.
NIV.22:
All his laws are before me;
I have not turned away from his decrees.
NIV.23:
I have been blameless before him
and have kept myself from sin.
NIV.24:
The Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness,
according to the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
NIV.25:
To the faithful you show yourself faithful,
to the blameless you show yourself blameless,
NIV.26:
to the pure you show yourself pure,
but to the crooked you show yourself shrewd.
NIV.27:
You save the humble
but bring low those whose eyes are haughty.
NIV.28:
You, O Lord, keep my lamp burning;
my God turns my darkness into light.
NIV.29:
With your help I can advance against a troop;
with my God I can scale a wall.
NIV.30:
As for God, his way is perfect;
the word of the Lord is flawless.
He is a shield
for all who take refuge in him.
NIV.31:
For who is God besides the Lord?
And who is the Rock except our God?
NIV.32:
It is God who arms me with strength
and makes my way perfect.
NIV.33:
He makes my feet like the feet of a deer;
he enables me to stand on the heights.
NIV.34:
He trains my hands for battle;
my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
NIV.35:
You give me your shield of victory,
and your right hand sustains me;
you stoop down to make me great.
NIV.36:
You broaden the path beneath me,
so that my ankles do not turn.
NIV.37:
I pursued my enemies and overtook them;
I did not turn back till they were destroyed.
NIV.38:
I crushed them so that they could not rise;
they fell beneath my feet.
NIV.39:
You armed me with strength for battle;
you made my adversaries bow at my feet.
NIV.40:
You made my enemies turn their backs in flight,
and I destroyed my foes.
NIV.41:
They cried for help, but there was no one to save them—
to the Lord, but he did not answer.
NIV.42:
I beat them as fine as dust borne on the wind;
I poured them out like mud in the streets.
NIV.43:
You have delivered me from the attacks of the people;
you have made me the head of nations;
people I did not know are subject to me.
NIV.44:
As soon as they hear me, they obey me;
foreigners cringe before me.
NIV.45:
They all lose heart;
they come trembling from their strongholds.
NIV.46:
The Lord lives! Praise be to my Rock!
Exalted be God my Savior!
NIV.47:
He is the God who avenges me,
who subdues nations under me,
NIV.48:
who saves me from my enemies.
You exalted me above my foes;
from violent men you rescued me.
NIV.49:
Therefore I will praise you among the nations, O Lord;
I will sing praises to your name.
NIV.50:
He gives his king great victories;
he shows unfailing kindness to his anointed,
to David and his descendants forever.

NRSVCE.1: I love you, O LORD, my strength.
NRSVCE.2:
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer,
my God, my rock in whom I take refuge,
my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
NRSVCE.3:
I call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised,
so I shall be saved from my enemies.
NRSVCE.4:
The cords of death encompassed me;
the torrents of perdition assailed me;
NRSVCE.5:
the cords of Sheol entangled me;
the snares of death confronted me.
NRSVCE.6:
In my distress I called upon the LORD;
to my God I cried for help.
From his temple he heard my voice,
and my cry to him reached his ears.
NRSVCE.7:
Then the earth reeled and rocked;
the foundations also of the mountains trembled
and quaked, because he was angry.
NRSVCE.8:
Smoke went up from his nostrils,
and devouring fire from his mouth;
glowing coals flamed forth from him.
NRSVCE.9:
He bowed the heavens, and came down;
thick darkness was under his feet.
NRSVCE.10:
He rode on a cherub, and flew;
he came swiftly upon the wings of the wind.
NRSVCE.11:
He made darkness his covering around him,
his canopy thick clouds dark with water.
NRSVCE.12:
Out of the brightness before him
there broke through his clouds
hailstones and coals of fire.
NRSVCE.13:
The LORD also thundered in the heavens,
and the Most High uttered his voice.
NRSVCE.14:
And he sent out his arrows, and scattered them;
he flashed forth lightnings, and routed them.
NRSVCE.15:
Then the channels of the sea were seen,
and the foundations of the world were laid bare
at your rebuke, O LORD,
at the blast of the breath of your nostrils.
NRSVCE.16:
He reached down from on high, he took me;
he drew me out of mighty waters.
NRSVCE.17:
He delivered me from my strong enemy,
and from those who hated me;
for they were too mighty for me.
NRSVCE.18:
They confronted me in the day of my calamity;
but the LORD was my support.
NRSVCE.19:
He brought me out into a broad place;
he delivered me, because he delighted in me.
NRSVCE.20:
The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness;
according to the cleanness of my hands he recompensed me.
NRSVCE.21:
For I have kept the ways of the LORD,
and have not wickedly departed from my God.
NRSVCE.22:
For all his ordinances were before me,
and his statutes I did not put away from me.
NRSVCE.23:
I was blameless before him,
and I kept myself from guilt.
NRSVCE.24:
Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness,
according to the cleanness of my hands in his sight.
NRSVCE.25:
With the loyal you show yourself loyal;
with the blameless you show yourself blameless;
NRSVCE.26:
with the pure you show yourself pure;
and with the crooked you show yourself perverse.
NRSVCE.27:
For you deliver a humble people,
but the haughty eyes you bring down.
NRSVCE.28:
It is you who light my lamp;
the LORD, my God, lights up my darkness.
NRSVCE.29:
By you I can crush a troop,
and by my God I can leap over a wall.
NRSVCE.30:
This God — his way is perfect;
the promise of the LORD proves true;
he is a shield for all who take refuge in him.
NRSVCE.31:
For who is God except the LORD?
And who is a rock besides our God? —
NRSVCE.32:
the God who girded me with strength,
and made my way safe.
NRSVCE.33:
He made my feet like the feet of a deer,
and set me secure on the heights.
NRSVCE.34:
He trains my hands for war,
so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
NRSVCE.35:
You have given me the shield of your salvation,
and your right hand has supported me;
your help has made me great.
NRSVCE.36:
You gave me a wide place for my steps under me,
and my feet did not slip.
NRSVCE.37:
I pursued my enemies and overtook them;
and did not turn back until they were consumed.
NRSVCE.38:
I struck them down, so that they were not able to rise;
they fell under my feet.
NRSVCE.39:
For you girded me with strength for the battle;
you made my assailants sink under me.
NRSVCE.40:
You made my enemies turn their backs to me,
and those who hated me I destroyed.
NRSVCE.41:
They cried for help, but there was no one to save them;
they cried to the LORD, but he did not answer them.
NRSVCE.42:
I beat them fine, like dust before the wind;
I cast them out like the mire of the streets.
NRSVCE.43:
You delivered me from strife with the peoples;
you made me head of the nations;
people whom I had not known served me.
NRSVCE.44:
As soon as they heard of me they obeyed me;
foreigners came cringing to me.
NRSVCE.45:
Foreigners lost heart,
and came trembling out of their strongholds.
NRSVCE.46:
The LORD lives! Blessed be my rock,
and exalted be the God of my salvation,
NRSVCE.47:
the God who gave me vengeance
and subdued peoples under me;
NRSVCE.48:
who delivered me from my enemies;
indeed, you exalted me above my adversaries;
you delivered me from the violent.
NRSVCE.49:
For this I will extol you, O LORD, among the nations,
and sing praises to your name.
NRSVCE.50:
Great triumphs he gives to his king,
and shows steadfast love to his anointed,
to David and his descendants forever.

NET.1: He said: “I love you, LORD, my source of strength!
NET.2: The LORD is my high ridge, my stronghold, my deliverer. My God is my rocky summit where I take shelter, my shield, the horn that saves me, and my refuge.
NET.3: I called to the LORD, who is worthy of praise, and I was delivered from my enemies.
NET.4: The waves of death engulfed me, the currents of chaos overwhelmed me.
NET.5: The ropes of Sheol tightened around me, the snares of death trapped me.
NET.6: In my distress I called to the LORD; I cried out to my God. From his heavenly temple he heard my voice; he listened to my cry for help.
NET.7: The earth heaved and shook; the roots of the mountains trembled; they heaved because he was angry.
NET.8: Smoke ascended from his nose; fire devoured as it came from his mouth; he hurled down fiery coals.
NET.9: He made the sky sink as he descended; a thick cloud was under his feet.
NET.10: He mounted a winged angel and flew; he glided on the wings of the wind.
NET.11: He shrouded himself in darkness, in thick rain clouds.
NET.12: From the brightness in front of him came hail and fiery coals.
NET.13: The LORD thundered in the sky; the sovereign One shouted.
NET.14: He shot his arrows and scattered them, many lightning bolts and routed them.
NET.15: The depths of the sea were exposed; the inner regions of the world were uncovered by your battle cry, LORD, by the powerful breath from your nose.
NET.16: He reached down from above and took hold of me; he pulled me from the surging water.
NET.17: He rescued me from my strong enemy, from those who hate me, for they were too strong for me.
NET.18: They confronted me in my day of calamity, but the LORD helped me.
NET.19: He brought me out into a wide open place; he delivered me because he was pleased with me.
NET.20: The LORD repaid me for my godly deeds; he rewarded my blameless behavior.
NET.21: For I have obeyed the LORD’s commands; I have not rebelled against my God.
NET.22: For I am aware of all his regulations, and I do not reject his rules.
NET.23: I was innocent before him, and kept myself from sinning.
NET.24: The LORD rewarded me for my godly deeds; he took notice of my blameless behavior.
NET.25: You prove to be loyal to one who is faithful; you prove to be trustworthy to one who is innocent.
NET.26: You prove to be reliable to one who is blameless, but you prove to be deceptive to one who is perverse.
NET.27: For you deliver oppressed people, but you bring down those who have a proud look.
NET.28: Indeed, you are my lamp, LORD. My God illuminates the darkness around me.
NET.29: Indeed, with your help I can charge against an army; by my God’s power I can jump over a wall.
NET.30: The one true God acts in a faithful manner; the LORD’s promise is reliable; he is a shield to all who take shelter in him.
NET.31: Indeed, who is God besides the LORD? Who is a protector besides our God?
NET.32: The one true God gives me strength; he removes the obstacles in my way.
NET.33: He gives me the agility of a deer; he enables me to negotiate the rugged terrain.
NET.34: He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend even the strongest bow.
NET.35: You give me your protective shield; your right hand supports me; your willingness to help enables me to prevail.
NET.36: You widen my path; my feet do not slip.
NET.37: I chase my enemies and catch them; I do not turn back until I wipe them out.
NET.38: I beat them to death; they fall at my feet.
NET.39: You give me strength for battle; you make my foes kneel before me.
NET.40: You make my enemies retreat; I destroy those who hate me.
NET.41: They cry out, but there is no one to help them; they cry out to the LORD, but he does not answer them.
NET.42: I grind them as fine windblown dust; I beat them underfoot like clay in the streets.
NET.43: You rescue me from a hostile army; you make me a leader of nations; people over whom I had no authority are now my subjects.
NET.44: When they hear of my exploits, they submit to me. Foreigners are powerless before me;
NET.45: foreigners lose their courage; they shake with fear as they leave their strongholds.
NET.46: The LORD is alive! My protector is praiseworthy! The God who delivers me is exalted as king!
NET.47: The one true God completely vindicates me; he makes nations submit to me.
NET.48: He delivers me from my enemies; you snatch me away from those who attack me; you rescue me from violent men.
NET.49: So I will give you thanks before the nations, O LORD! I will sing praises to you!
NET.50: He gives his chosen king magnificent victories; he is faithful to his chosen ruler, to David and his descendants forever.”
